name: Brent Crude
summary: Day 63 Thursday — BRENT CLOSES $118-120 (+6-8% 1D), HIGHEST SINCE JUNE 2022; $115/Branch-1-REACTIVATION TRIGGER NOW DAY 1 CONFIRMED (sustained-close criterion met for first session). Wire-confirmed front-month: TradingEconomics $120.30 (+8.13%), CNBC $118.03 (+6.0%) close, intraday print $120 — yesterday's 19:30-IST estimate of $114.64 close was UNDER actual; the magnitude of the move was nearly double the prior day's print. Drivers: (a) Trump told Axios he REJECTS Iran's Hormuz-First proposal — *'the blockade is somewhat more effective than the bombing. They are choking like a stuffed pig'* — formally answering 'extended economic warfare as base case' on the record (CONFIRMED Axios primary; Bloomberg, NBC, CBS, NewRepublic corroborated); (b) CENTCOM 'short and powerful' strike plan PREPARED-BUT-NOT-ACTIVATED (Axios; preparation-not-action); (c) EIA Apr 29 inventory data: SHARP DRAWS in US crude/fuel stocks; US EXPORTS surged to record >6M bpd (operational confirmation of physical tightness); (d) UAE OPEC exit takes effect May 1 (calendar inflection, ~24 hours away); (e) Iran rial 1.8M/USD black market — the empirical validation of the 'blockade > bombing' strategy (Bloomberg headline literally 'US Naval Blockade Pushes Iran's Currency to Record Low'). Brent-WTI spread NARROWED $15→$13 — WTI catches Brent for first time, US export-arbitrage compressing the spread (a STRUCTURAL RESOLUTION CHANNEL, not Hormuz-resolution-driven). yfinance roll-distortion warning: BZ=F shows $111.92 / +0.6% (June contract base) — IGNORE; use wire $118-120/+6-8%. [TRUNCATED — needs re-synthesis]
